title: Veggie Fried Rice with Egg
description: A quick and easy one-pan meal packed with colorful vegetables and protein, perfect for a family dinner. This recipe is designed to be kid-friendly and uses minimal dishes.
instructions: Chop carrots and broccoli into small, kid-friendly pieces. Mince garlic and ginger. Slice green onions, separating white and green parts.
Heat 1 tbsp vegetable oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat. Add beaten eggs and scramble until cooked through. Remove from skillet and set aside.
Add remaining 1 tbsp vegetable oil to the skillet. Add carrots, broccoli, garlic, and ginger. Stir-fry for 3-5 minutes until vegetables are tender-crisp.
Add day-old rice and frozen peas to the skillet. Break up any clumps of rice. Stir-fry for 3-4 minutes, ensuring rice is heated through and slightly crispy.
Pour in soy sauce and sesame oil. Add the scrambled eggs and the white parts of the green onions. Stir well to combine all ingredients and flavors.
Season with salt and pepper to taste. Garnish with the green parts of the green onions before serving.
tips: Using day-old, cold rice is key for fried rice; it prevents the dish from becoming mushy.
Don't overcrowd the pan. If you're making a larger batch, cook in two separate batches to ensure even cooking and browning.
For extra protein, you can add cooked chicken or tofu along with the eggs.
